narrator.description: A friendly, clear, and encouraging adult voice.

droplet_drip.description: A small, cheerful, and curious water droplet with a youthful voice.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Hello, young adventurers! Today, we're going on an amazing journey with a tiny water drop. Our friend, Droplet Drip, will show us how water travels all around our Earth. Are you ready?

segments.speaker: droplet_drip
segments.text: Wow, it's getting warm! The sun is shining on me in the big ocean. I feel lighter and lighter. When water gets warm enough, it turns into a gas called 'water vapor' and floats up. This is called 'evaporation'!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Droplet Drip, now water vapor, rises high into the sky. Up there, it's much colder! When water vapor cools down, it turns back into tiny liquid water drops. These little drops gather together to form big, fluffy 'clouds'. This process is called 'condensation'.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.question_text: What is the name for when water gets warm and turns into a gas that floats up into the sky?
segments.acceptable_explanation: That's right! When water gets warm and turns into a gas, it's called evaporation. Great job!
segments.hint_text: Think about when the sun makes puddles disappear. What is that process called?

segments.speaker: droplet_drip
segments.text: Woohoo! I'm in a cloud now! But we're getting so heavy. When too many water drops join together in a cloud, they fall back down to Earth. This is called 'precipitation'! Sometimes I fall as rain, other times as snow or even hail. Splish, splash!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not quite, but that's okay! The process when water gets warm and turns into a gas is called 'evaporation'. Now, back to our adventure! Droplet Drip is in a cloud, and it's getting very full. When enough water gathers, it falls back to Earth as 'precipitation'.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Droplet Drip falls from the sky and lands in a small puddle. Then, the puddle flows into a stream, the stream into a river, and the river carries Droplet Drip all the way back to the big, wide ocean. The journey starts all over again!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.question_text: When water falls from the sky, what are some different forms it can take?
segments.acceptable_explanation: Exactly! Water can fall as rain, snow, or hail. You remembered!
segments.hint_text: Think about what comes down from clouds in different kinds of weather.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: What an amazing trip! You've learned how water evaporates from the ocean, condenses into clouds, and then precipitates back to Earth as rain, snow, or hail. This whole journey is called the 'water cycle'. Droplet Drip is ready for another adventure, and so are we!

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Almost! Water can fall as rain, snow, or hail. You've learned so much today! Water evaporates from the ocean, condenses into clouds, and then precipitates back to Earth. This never-ending journey is called the 'water cycle'. Thank you for joining Droplet Drip on this adventure!
